title: 2026 Oscar Nominations: Best Picture Race Analysis
text: Slate's analysis reveals the Oscar nominations showed an unexpected shift in the Best Picture race. Rather than the anticipated showdown between One Battle After Another and Hamnet, the competition narrowed to Sinners versus One Battle After Another. Sinners set a new record with 16 Oscar nominations, surpassing the previous high of 14 held by All About Eve, Titanic, and La La Land. The film received nominations across major categories including Best Picture, Director, and Actor, plus unexpected nods in Visual Effects and Supporting Actor for Delroy Lindo's long-overdue first Oscar nomination. Despite winning Best Drama at the Golden Globes, Hamnet faced significant setbacks. The film notably missed nominations for Best Editing and Best Cinematography, categories considered crucial predictors of Best Picture success. Only two movies have won Best Picture without netting at least a nomination for editing in the past 45 years. On betting markets, Sinners surged from 7% to 22% Best Picture probability, while One Battle declined from 72% to approximately that range, and Hamnet dropped from 13% to 4%.
